people.d: People are groups of humans that live together.
people.e: There are many people in the park today.

part.d: A piece or section of something bigger
part.e: The car has many different parts.

place.e: My favorite place to play is the park.

please.d: A nice way to ask for something
please.e: Can I have a cookie, please?

put.d: To place or set something somewhere
put.e: Can you put your toys away before dinner?

play.d: To have fun and enjoy yourself
play.e: We like to play outside at recess.

public.e: The park is a public place anyone can visit.

person.e: My best friend is a kind person.

point.e: I made a good point in my school project.

power.d: The ability to do something, like having energy or control
power.e: The power went out during the storm.

party.d: A fun event with friends and family
party.e: We are having a birthday party this weekend.

problem.d: something difficult that needs to be solved
problem.e: I had a problem with my bike, so I asked my dad for help.

president.d: the leader of a country or organization
president.e: The president of the United States lives in the White House.

possible.d: Something that can happen or be done
possible.e: It's possible for me to ride my bike to school.

political.d: relating to how a country or city is run
political.e: The mayor makes important political decisions for our city.

pay.e: I get paid to help my parents.

post.d: A message or picture you share online
post.e: I posted a picture of my dog.

past.e: In the past, I didn't know how to ride a bike.

playing.e: I enjoy playing with my friends outside.

pretty.d: Nice to look at or pleasing
pretty.e: The sunset is so pretty tonight.

probably.d: Most likely or expected to happen
probably.e: I will probably finish my homework tonight.

police.d: People who keep the community safe
police.e: The police officer helped the lost child find their parents.

period.e: I have swim practice for a period of one hour.

process.d: A series of steps to do something
process.e: The process of making a cake takes a lot of time.

plan.d: A way to do something in the future
plan.e: I have a plan to go to the park after school.

phone.d: A device used to talk to people far away
phone.e: I called my grandma on the phone yesterday.

position.d: Where someone or something is located
position.e: The player took his position on the field.

players.d: People who take part in a game or sport
players.e: The soccer players ran quickly down the field.

present.d: Giving a gift or being here now
present.e: The birthday party guests gave the child many presents.

problems.d: Difficult situations that need to be solved or fixed
problems.e: My math problems are tricky, but I'm going to keep trying.

program.d: A set of instructions that tells a computer what to do
program.e: My favorite computer program is the one that lets me draw pictures.

parents.d: The people who take care of you and raise you
parents.e: My parents help me with my homework every night.

personal.d: Something that belongs only to you
personal.e: That bike is my personal property.

played.d: To do an activity for fun
played.e: The children played on the swings at the park.

points.d: The score or number of things you get
points.e: The team earned 10 points for winning the game.

price.e: The price of a new bike is $100.

press.d: To use force to make something flat or smooth
press.e: I need to press my shirt before school.

private.d: Just for one person or a small group
private.e: My diary is a private book that only I read.

production.d: The act of making or creating something
production.e: The factory increased its production of toys.

project.d: A special task or piece of work
project.e: We have a class project to build a model car.

policy.d: A set of rules or guidelines
policy.e: The school has a policy about no running in the halls.

picture.e: I took a picture of my dog with my camera.

page.d: One side of a sheet of paper in a book.
page.e: The student turned the page to read the next chapter.

performance.d: When someone does something well, like acting or singing.
performance.e: The dancer's performance was amazing to watch.

player.d: Someone who takes part in a game or sport.
player.e: The soccer player scored the winning goal.

population.d: The number of people living in a place
population.e: The population of our city has grown a lot in the last few years.

provide.d: To give or supply something that is needed
provide.e: The store was able to provide the item I was looking for.

perfect.e: My drawing is perfect for the school project.

poor.d: Not having enough money or things.
poor.e: The poor family needs help from the community.

park.d: A big outdoor space for people to enjoy
park.e: We go to the park to play on the swings.

paid.d: When you get money for doing a job or activity.
paid.e: The boy was paid for mowing the lawn.

particular.d: Something that is special or important.
particular.e: The teacher asked the student a particular question.

parts.d: The different pieces that make up a whole thing.
parts.e: The robot has many different parts.

perhaps.d: A word used when you are not sure about something.
perhaps.e: Perhaps it will rain later today.

property.d: Something that belongs to a person or group.
property.e: The house and yard are the family's property.

published.d: When a book, newspaper, or other writing is made public.
published.e: The author's new book was just published.

practice.d: Doing something again and again to get better at it
practice.e: I practice my piano every day after school.

pass.d: To go by or move to the next thing
pass.e: I will pass the ball to my teammate during the game.

paul.e: My friend Paul is coming over to play later.

peace.d: When everything is calm and quiet
peace.e: I feel at peace when I am reading a book.

paper.d: Thin sheets of material to write or draw on
paper.e: I need to buy more paper to do my homework

pick.e: I get to pick which flavor of ice cream I want.

places.e: We visited many fun places on our family vacation.

particularly.d: Especially or more so than usual
particularly.e: I particularly enjoy playing outside on sunny days.

plans.d: a set of ideas to do something in the future
plans.e: My family has plans to go to the park this weekend.

popular.d: something that many people like or want
popular.e: The new toy is very popular with all the kids.

potential.d: something that could happen in the future
potential.e: The new student has a lot of potential to do well in school.

pressure.d: a feeling of being pushed to do something
pressure.e: The coach put pressure on the team to win the game.

pain.d: When your body feels hurt or uncomfortable
pain.e: I had a pain in my stomach after I ate too much.

previous.e: I went to the park yesterday, which was the previous day.

provided.e: The teacher provided the students with new pencils.

percent.d: A percent is a way to show a part of a whole.
percent.e: I got 90 percent of the answers correct on my test.

positive.d: thinking in a good and hopeful way
positive.e: My mom always tries to be positive and cheerful.

physical.e: The athlete does a lot of physical training.

piece.e: I want a piece of the birthday cake.

products.d: Things that are made and sold
products.e: The store sells many different products.

professional.d: Doing a job as a career, not just for fun
professional.e: The chef is a professional who cooks for a living.

paris.e: My dream is to visit Paris and see the Eiffel Tower.

posted.d: to put something online or on a wall for others to see
posted.e: She posted a picture of her birthday party on social media.

product.d: something that is made and sold
product.e: The new toy was a popular product for the store.

pre.e: Pre-school is for children before they start regular school.

purpose.d: The reason why something is done or made
purpose.e: The purpose of our field trip is to learn about nature.

passed.d: When something or someone goes by or comes to an end.
passed.e: The school bus passed by our house this morning.

photo.d: A picture taken with a camera.
photo.e: My mom took a photo of me on my birthday.

produced.d: When something is made or created.
produced.e: The factory produced many toys for the holiday season.

parliament.d: a group that makes laws for a country
parliament.e: The parliament in my country meets to discuss new laws.

prime.d: the best or most important part of something
prime.e: The prime time to watch my favorite show is in the evening.

parties.d: a special event where people gather to have fun
parties.e: The kids went to a birthday party with cake and games.

peter.e: Peter loved to play outside and catch bugs.

patients.d: people who are sick or injured and being cared for
patients.e: The doctor sees many patients every day.

pictures.e: The book has beautiful pictures of animals.

planning.d: Thinking ahead and making a plan to do something.
planning.e: I'm planning to make cookies this weekend.

plant.d: A living thing that grows in the ground, like a flower or a tree.
plant.e: My mom planted a new rose bush in our garden.

primary.d: The most important or main one.
primary.e: My primary goal is to get good grades this year.

politics.d: The way a country or community is run and governed.
politics.e: The mayor talked about politics at the town meeting.

protect.d: To keep someone or something safe from harm.
protect.e: The firefighters protect the people in the neighborhood.

placed.d: To put something in a certain spot
placed.e: I placed my books on the table.

professor.d: A teacher at a university or college
professor.e: The professor taught the science class.

programs.d: A set of instructions for a computer

plus.d: Another word for 'and' or 'in addition to'
plus.e: I like chocolate plus vanilla ice cream.

powerful.d: Something that is very strong and can do a lot
powerful.e: The lion is a powerful animal.

prices.d: The amount of money something costs
prices.e: The prices at the store went up this week.

pro.d: Someone who is very good at something
pro.e: The football player is a pro at throwing the ball.

previously.d: Something that happened before the current time.
previously.e: I previously went to the park, but now I'm at home.

protection.d: Things that keep you safe or secure.
protection.e: The umbrella provides protection from the rain.

partner.d: Someone you work or play with
partner.e: My partner and I built a fort in the backyard.

putting.d: Placing something in a certain place
putting.e: I am putting the toys back in the box.

performed.d: When you do or carry out an action or task.
performed.e: The magician performed amazing tricks on stage.

plays.d: When someone acts out a story or pretends to be a character.
plays.e: My little sister loves to play with her dolls.

pm.d: The time of day from noon until midnight.
pm.e: We have soccer practice at 4 pm every Tuesday.

pop.d: A type of lively, upbeat music.
pop.e: My favorite pop song is playing on the radio.

port.d: A place by the ocean where ships can load and unload.
port.e: The big ships come into the busy port to deliver their cargo.

presence.d: When you are in a place or able to be seen.
presence.e: The teacher's presence in the classroom helps the students learn.

prior.e: I need to finish my homework prior to going to the park.

prison.d: A place where people go when they break the law and need to be kept safe
prison.e: The police officer took the man to the prison after he stole a car.

produce.d: Fruits and vegetables that are grown on farms
produce.e: The grocery store has fresh produce like apples, carrots, and lettuce.

profile.d: A picture or description of someone that shows what they look like
profile.e: The artist drew a profile of the person's face.

progress.d: When you get better at something or move forward
progress.e: The student made a lot of progress in her reading this year.

presented.d: To show or offer something to someone
presented.e: The teacher presented the award to the student.

prevent.e: The fence was put up to prevent the dog from escaping.

prepared.e: I prepared my backpack for the first day of school.

prince.d: A special title for a boy or man who is part of a royal family
prince.e: The prince lives in a big castle with his family.

provides.d: To give something to someone or make something available
provides.e: The store provides snacks and drinks for the customers.

pull.d: To make something move towards you by using force
pull.e: I can pull the wagon with my friends inside.

prove.d: To show that something is true or correct.
prove.e: She had to prove that she finished her homework.

push.d: To use force to move something away from you.
push.e: He had to push the door open because it was stuck.

patient.d: Someone who is calm and waits quietly.
patient.e: The patient dog sat by the door, waiting for its owner to come home.

photos.d: Pictures that are taken with a camera.
photos.e: I took lots of photos on my trip to the zoo.

plants.d: Living things that grow in the ground, like trees, flowers, and vegetables.
plants.e: The farmer grows many different plants on his farm.

proud.d: Feeling good about something you did or someone you know.
proud.e: Sarah was proud when she won the race.

path.d: A way to get from one place to another.
path.e: I follow the path through the park to get home.

planned.d: To think about and decide how something will happen.
planned.e: The family planned a trip to the beach.

powers.d: The special abilities that someone or something has.

plane.d: a flat surface used for traveling in the air
plane.e: The plane flew over the clouds.

possibly.e: I might possibly visit the park today.

pieces.d: small parts that fit together to make a whole
pieces.e: The jigsaw puzzle has hundreds of little pieces that need to be put together.

profit.d: the money a business makes after paying all its costs
profit.e: The bakery made a good profit from selling all the cookies and cakes.

proper.d: correct or right in a particular situation
proper.e: The student used proper grammar when writing her essay.

promise.d: to say you will definitely do something
promise.e: I promised my mom I would clean my room this weekend.

province.d: a region or area within a country
province.e: Ontario is a province in the country of Canada.

persons.e: There were many persons at the park today.

phase.d: A stage or part of something that happens over time
phase.e: The moon goes through different phases each month.

pair.e: I bought a new pair of shoes for school.

picked.e: The teacher picked the best drawing to put on the wall.

paying.d: Using money to buy or get something.
paying.e: She is paying for her new bike with her allowance.

policies.d: The rules that a company, organization, or government follows.
policies.e: The school has policies about cell phone use.

pool.d: A small area of water for swimming or playing in
pool.e: I love to swim in the pool on hot summer days.

providing.d: To give someone or something what they need
providing.e: My mom is providing me with healthy food for lunch.

perform.d: To perform means to do a show or act in front of people.
perform.e: The dancers will perform a ballet on the stage.

possibility.d: A possibility is something that could happen or be true.
possibility.e: There is a possibility of rain today, so I'll bring an umbrella.

purchase.d: To purchase means to buy something with money.
purchase.e: I purchased a new book at the store yesterday.

planet.d: a large object in space that circles the sun
planet.e: Earth is the planet we live on.

positions.d: where something or someone is placed
positions.e: The players took their positions on the field.

proof.e: The photo is proof that I visited the park.

pages.d: the pieces of paper in a book
pages.e: I read 20 pages of my book last night.

programme.d: A plan or schedule of events
programme.e: The school has a special programme for students to learn about science.

passing.e: I saw a car passing by on the street as I was walking home.

personnel.d: the people who work at a place
personnel.e: The school's personnel include the teachers, principal, and office staff.

plenty.d: a large amount or more than enough
plenty.e: There was plenty of cake left over after the party.

pulled.d: to move something by holding it and forcing it to come towards you
pulled.e: I pulled the door open to go outside.

pack.d: A group of things that are tied or held together
pack.e: I brought a pack of crayons to color with.

permanent.d: Something that lasts a very long time
permanent.e: The tattoo is a permanent mark on my skin.

properties.d: The things that belong to something
properties.e: The house has many nice properties.

pleasure.d: Pleasure is when you feel happy and enjoy something.
pleasure.e: It's a pleasure to play with my friends at the park.

properly.d: Properly means doing something the right or correct way.
properly.e: I made sure to properly clean my room before my parents got home.

perspective.d: The way you look at or think about something
perspective.e: Looking at the problem from a different perspective helped me solve it

pilot.d: The person who flies an airplane
pilot.e: The pilot carefully landed the plane on the runway

platform.d: A raised area where people can stand or sit
platform.e: The train stopped at the platform so the passengers could get off

principles.d: Important rules or beliefs that guide your actions
principles.e: One of my principles is to always be kind to others

pure.d: Completely clean and without any bad things mixed in.
pure.e: The water in the river is pure and fresh.

purposes.d: The reasons why you do something.
purposes.e: The main purpose of a school is to help students learn.

prize.d: a reward for doing something well
prize.e: The winner received a special prize.

papers.d: Thin sheets of material used for writing or printing
papers.e: I need to sign some important papers today.

parent.d: A mother or father of a child
parent.e: My parents help me with my homework.

perfectly.d: Something that is completely right or correct.

portion.d: A part or section of something larger.
portion.e: I will save a portion of my ice cream for later.

principal.d: The most important or main person in charge.
principal.e: The principal of my school makes all the big decisions.

print.d: To write or make letters, numbers, or images on paper.
print.e: I will print my homework assignment before class.

pacific.e: The ocean is pacific and beautiful.

processes.d: A series of actions that produce a result
processes.e: The machine uses many processes to make the product.

partners.d: Two or more people who work together.
partners.e: The science project partners worked hard.

prepare.e: I need to prepare my backpack for school tomorrow.

primarily.e: Cats are primarily active at night.

procedure.d: A set of steps to do something.
procedure.e: The procedure for washing dishes is to scrub, rinse, and dry.

painting.e: The painting of the sunset was beautiful.

payment.e: I made a payment on my new bike.

pc.e: I do my homework on the PC.

percentage.d: a part of a whole shown as a number
percentage.e: I got 90% on my math test.

poverty.d: when someone does not have enough money

practical.e: Comfortable shoes are a practical choice.

principle.e: Honesty is one of my main principles.

promote.e: The company will promote the new product.

palace.d: A very large and fancy house where a king or queen lives
palace.e: The princess lives in a beautiful palace.

philosophy.d: The study of big questions about life and the world
philosophy.e: My teacher talked about the philosophy of right and wrong.

practices.d: Things you do over and over to get better at them
practices.e: The soccer team practices every day to improve their skills.

prefer.d: To like something better than something else
prefer.e: I prefer chocolate ice cream over vanilla.

princess.d: A young girl who is the daughter of a king or queen
princess.e: The princess waved to the people from the castle window.

panel.d: A flat surface that controls or shows information.
panel.e: The control panel had many different buttons and switches.

personality.d: The way a person thinks, feels, and behaves.
personality.e: The friendly dog had a great personality.

politicians.d: People who work in the government.
politicians.e: The politicians worked hard to help their community.

pounds.d: A unit of weight, like for measuring how heavy something is.
pounds.e: The heavy box weighed ten pounds.

pattern.e: The rug had a beautiful floral pattern.

pregnant.d: when a woman's body is growing a baby
pregnant.e: My aunt is pregnant and will have a new baby soon.

promised.e: The teacher promised to give us extra time on the test.

proposal.d: a plan or idea that is suggested
proposal.e: The class had a great proposal for the school play.

puts.d: to place or set something somewhere
puts.e: The chef puts the cake in the oven to bake.

performing.d: Doing a show or play for people to watch
performing.e: The dancers were amazing while performing on stage.

plate.d: A flat dish used to hold food
plate.e: I put my sandwich on a clean plate to eat.

plot.d: A plan for a story or play
plot.e: The plot of the movie was very exciting.

pope.d: The leader of the Catholic Church
pope.e: The pope visited our city last year.

posts.d: Wooden or metal pieces that hold things up
posts.e: They put up new posts to make a fence.

premier.d: The most important or main one
premier.e: The premier soccer team in our town is very good.

programming.d: Writing instructions for a computer to follow
programming.e: I learned how to do some basic programming in my computer class.

proved.e: They proved the new game was lots of fun to play.

publication.d: A book, magazine, or newspaper that is printed
publication.e: My favorite magazine is a great publication.

permission.d: Saying it's okay to do something
permission.e: I asked my mom for permission to go to the park.

plastic.d: A strong, flexible material used to make many things
plastic.e: My new water bottle is made of sturdy plastic.

protected.d: Kept safe from harm or danger
protected.e: The wildlife in the park is carefully protected.

pakistan.e: My friend's family is from Pakistan.

peak.e: The mountain has a tall peak covered in snow.

personally.e: I personally like to play outside after school.

pointed.e: The pencil has a pointed tip.

paint.d: a liquid that is used to color or decorate something
paint.e: We used paint to make the walls look bright.

pants.d: a piece of clothing that covers your legs
pants.e: I put on my favorite pair of pants to go to school.

parking.d: the place where you leave your car when you are not using it
parking.e: We found a good parking spot near the store.
